title: Listening - Part 2
description: You will hear a man called James talking about helping at a community dance festival. For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.
instructions: You will hear a man called James talking about helping at a community dance festival. For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

extracts.context: A man called James talks about helping at a community dance festival.
extracts.audio_script: Good afternoon. I’m James, and I organise volunteer opportunities for Moving Steps, our town’s annual community dance festival. The festival started when a small dance class wanted to perform for relatives and friends, but it soon grew to include schools, adult groups and visiting professionals. Our main office is inside a converted [9]ticket office[/9] at the old railway station. The trains still run, but this unused room has been redesigned with desks and storage space, and it is a convenient place for performers arriving from other towns to collect information.||The festival includes ballet, street dance, traditional dance and performances designed for children. Last year, the largest audiences attended the street-dance shows. This year, according to advance bookings, the most popular part of the programme is [10]contemporary dance[/10], especially a performance taking place outdoors beside the river. Volunteers are needed in several venues. Before helping, everyone attends a meeting. It contains practical information about schedules and entrances, but the key topic is [11]audience safety[/11], because performance areas can become crowded when large groups arrive and leave at the same time.||Our volunteers are not expected to dance or understand specialist techniques. First-time helpers usually begin at the [12]information point[/12], handing out maps and advising visitors where particular performances take place. Festival shirts are provided, so volunteers can be recognised easily. Please wear comfortable shoes, and bring a [13]small backpack[/13] rather than a large bag, as storage at the venues is very limited and you may move between locations during a shift. Drinks and a snack will be available for people working longer sessions.||We also give young people opportunities behind the scenes. Performers need music prepared carefully before they appear on stage, and this year a small student team will help organise the [14]sound files[/14] for each show under professional supervision. Another new feature is our free morning programme for families. Instead of inviting children simply to watch rehearsals, we are offering a [15]movement workshop[/15] where they can try simple activities with an instructor. It booked up within a week. Volunteers will welcome families and help check attendance.||Anyone helping for at least four shifts may attend one festival performance without charge using a [16]guest ticket[/16]. This is our way of recognising their work. At the end of the festival, performers and volunteers are invited to an informal [17]celebration party[/17], with music and refreshments but no formal awards. Finally, the festival hopes to become accessible to more people. Money raised through programme sales this year will help provide [18]sign language interpretation[/18] at selected shows next summer. Volunteer registration is open until Friday, and forms are available both online and at the station office.

dialogue.speaker: james
dialogue.text: Good afternoon. I’m James, and I organise volunteer opportunities for Moving Steps, our town’s annual community dance festival. The festival started when a small dance class wanted to perform for relatives and friends, but it soon grew to include schools, adult groups and visiting professionals. Our main office is inside a converted ticket office at the old railway station. The trains still run, but this unused room has been redesigned with desks and storage space, and it is a convenient place for performers arriving from other towns to collect information.||The festival includes ballet, street dance, traditional dance and performances designed for children. Last year, the largest audiences attended the street-dance shows. This year, according to advance bookings, the most popular part of the programme is contemporary dance, especially a performance taking place outdoors beside the river. Volunteers are needed in several venues. Before helping, everyone attends a meeting. It contains practical information about schedules and entrances, but the key topic is audience safety, because performance areas can become crowded when large groups arrive and leave at the same time.||Our volunteers are not expected to dance or understand specialist techniques. First-time helpers usually begin at the information point, handing out maps and advising visitors where particular performances take place. Festival shirts are provided, so volunteers can be recognised easily. Please wear comfortable shoes, and bring a small backpack rather than a large bag, as storage at the venues is very limited and you may move between locations during a shift. Drinks and a snack will be available for people working longer sessions.||We also give young people opportunities behind the scenes. Performers need music prepared carefully before they appear on stage, and this year a small student team will help organise the sound files for each show under professional supervision. Another new feature is our free morning programme for families. Instead of inviting children simply to watch rehearsals, we are offering a movement workshop where they can try simple activities with an instructor. It booked up within a week. Volunteers will welcome families and help check attendance.||Anyone helping for at least four shifts may attend one festival performance without charge using a guest ticket. This is our way of recognising their work. At the end of the festival, performers and volunteers are invited to an informal celebration party, with music and refreshments but no formal awards. Finally, the festival hopes to become accessible to more people. Money raised through programme sales this year will help provide sign language interpretation at selected shows next summer. Volunteer registration is open until Friday, and forms are available both online and at the station office.
